name: Israeli Folk Dance (Open Session)
description: An open level session of Israeli folk dancing featuring traditional and contemporary circles and high-energy group dances.

name: Buena Vista Social Club

name: Kolker

name: Dreamscape

name: Health and Social Services Committee
description: Meeting focused on local health services, social programs, and community welfare initiatives.

name: Mommy and Me Classes

description: Committee meeting covering health and human services, education, youth services, and cultural affairs.

name: City Services Planning Work Group
description: A community board meeting focused on planning and reviewing city services for the local district.

name: Parks, Recreation and Culture Committee

name: Public Safety Committee
description: Meeting with local agencies and residents to address neighborhood safety and quality-of-life concerns.

name: MAD for a Cause: Project Street Beat

description: A Friday morning exploration of northern Harlem, focusing on the cultural and historical landscape of Washington Heights and North Sugar Hill.
short_name: Washington Heights - Sugar Hill North Harlem Walking Tour

name: Forest Fridays @ Van Cortlandt Park
description: Join volunteers to save the forest floor by removing invasive species like Bush Honeysuckle and Multiflora Rose. This winter initiative is critical for protecting native plants while they are dormant.

name: Volunteering at Conference House Park - The Blue Wave
description: Participate in a self-directed beach and park clean-up initiative. Volunteers help remove debris and maintain the beauty of the Staten Island waterfront.

name: Ceramics Studio Time - 4-session access block
description: Flexible open studio access block for ceramic artists to work on independent projects with access to pottery wheels and hand-building tables. Clay must be purchased through the makerspace and glaze firing fees apply based on size.

name: Shelter from the Storm: An Artist’s Eye with Stan Allen and Polly Apfelbaum
description: Shelter from the Storm features a collaborative effort between Stan Allen and Polly Apfelbaum, blending architecture and contemporary art. The show examines thematic links between environment, protection, and spatial aesthetics.

name: WSCAH’s 86th Street Market
location: St. Paul & St. Andrew United Methodist Church
description: Each month, customers can receive a regular distribution of USDA MyPlate Balanced groceries. These packages contain a variety of healthy items, including grains, proteins, dairy products, and fresh fruits and vegetables.

name: Primeval Ground [deep, unfathomable]
description: A group exhibition featuring ten artists exploring monochrome and low-chrome artworks across media like photography, weaving, and sculpture. The exhibit includes a panel discussion on Jan 15 and a movement drawing workshop on Jan 24.

name: Somewhere Funny, Nowhere Serious!
description: Somewhere Nowhere NYC transforms into an intimate speakeasy-style comedy club featuring a live stand-up showcase with big-name comedians as seen on Netflix and HBO.

name: Winter Realm Cinema
description: Winter Realm Cinema is a seasonal rooftop film experience in Midtown featuring a fully enclosed and heated terrace with stunning Manhattan views. The program offers a curated selection of cult films, classics, and new releases enjoyed via state-of-the-art wireless headphones. Guests can also enjoy a full bar and classic cinema concessions like fresh popcorn and hot dogs through March 1st.

name: Eternal Nature: Immersive Digital Art Exhibition
description: Experience 'Eternal Nature,' a multisensory digital art exhibition featuring immersive landscapes like crashing waterfalls and blooming forests. The gallery also showcases a landmark collaboration with the Musée d'Orsay, reimagining masterpieces by Monet and Van Gogh through light, sound, and scent.

name: Karaoke Friday
description: Join the Gottscheer Hall community for their signature Friday night karaoke. Experience a wide selection of German beers and pub eats while enjoying or participating in an evening of singing and entertainment.

name: Live Music
description: Grand Vin hosts live music performances Wednesday through Saturday nights, creating a sophisticated ambiance for dining and drinks. Guests can enjoy a rotating selection of musicians while exploring the venue's curated wine list and Italian-inspired menu.

name: Friday Feel-Good Improv Jam
description: Kick off the weekend with a high-energy, supportive improv jam open to all. Whether you're a seasoned pro or a first-timer, this jam is all about having fun and making good choices on stage.

name: Character Counts Open Mic
description: Hosted by Liz Coin, this open mic is dedicated specifically to developing characters. Performers get five-minute slots to workshop existing characters or debut brand new ones.

name: UNIQLO Friday Nights
description: The museum stays open late every Friday night with free admission for New York State residents. Guests can enjoy exhibitions, drop-in drawing, film screenings, and unique shopping at the Design Store.

name: Smalls Afternoon Jam Session Hosted by Andrew Kushnir
description: An open jazz jam session featuring a professional house trio and hosted by drummer Andrew Kushnir. Musicians are invited to join the stage for an afternoon of communal music-making.
short_name: Smalls Afternoon Jam Session Hosted by Andrew Kushnir

name: Free Friday Night Hip Hop Dance Class & Party
description: Join Ailey Extension instructor TweetBoogie for a free hip hop dance class followed by a high-energy dance party featuring sets by DJ Bizzy. All ages and skill levels are welcome to participate in this vibrant Friday evening event.
short_name: Free Friday Night Hip Hop Dance Class & Party

name: Adult Cooking Class — Tasty Thai
description: Master the flavors of Thailand by preparing Pad Thai, Pineapple Chicken Satay with nut-free Thai sauce, and fresh veggie summer rolls. This BYOB workshop is entirely hands-on and designed for all skill levels.

name: Artist Studios: Eva Tellier
description: Visit artist Eva Tellier in her studio to explore her latest works and creative process. This recurring studio residency allows visitors to engage directly with contemporary makers.

name: Friday Night at MFAW
location: Music for a While Listening Bar & Lounge
description: Experience an immersive late-night music escape featuring stories and global sounds in an intimate underground setting. This Friday night session offers a unique listening bar atmosphere beneath the cosmopolitan heart of the city.

name: Joan Jonas: Moving off the Land II (Excerpts)
location: Columbia University Lenfest Center for the Arts
description: This multimedia exhibition features drawings of red fish by alumna Joan Jonas, scaled up into stunning banners. The installation explores the science and storytelling of the world's oceans as part of a yearlong 60th anniversary celebration.

name: A Night at the Museum: The Overnight Experience
description: Guests explore their favorite halls and fall asleep under the iconic Blue Whale or surrounded by other beloved Museum exhibits.

name: Seneca Village Educators' Tour
description: Join Central Park Conservancy guides for a tour exploring the history of the Seneca Village community, designed specifically for NYC educators. Participants will learn about the displacement of residents for the construction of the park.

name: Intro to MIG Welding: Half-Day Intensive - Brooklyn
description: Learn the fundamentals of Metal Inert Gas welding, the simplest form of joining steel. This hands-on workshop covers equipment setup, safety, and basic welding beads.

name: Joey Diaz: 62 & Still Slinging
description: Joey "Coco" Diaz brings his powerhouse stand-up comedy and legendary storytelling to the stage. Known for his wild and unpredictable performances, Diaz is a veteran of film, television, and hit podcasts.

name: Bookstore
description: A play exploring human connections and stories set within the quiet sanctuary of a bookstore environment.

name: Between Two Drummers I
description: This rhythm-focused showcase features dynamic percussion performances and intricate collaborative improvisation.

name: Public Tour of "With a Single Step"
description: A guided gallery tour of MOCA’s core exhibition providing an overview of Chinese history in America from the 19th century to the present.
short_name: Public Tour of "With a Single Step"

name: Winter Knitting with Hilary Snell
description: Build on your knitting foundation this winter in biweekly classes designed to improve your technique and craft.

name: Studio Signs: From Now: A Collection in Context
description: A discussion related to the exhibition "Studio Signs: From Now: A Collection in Context."
short_name: From Now: A Collection in Context

name: Robinson's Readers: Story Time and Craft
description: Little kids (and their grown-ups), join us for a monthly educator-facilitated picture book story time and hands-on craft inspired by the life and legacy of Jackie Robinson.
short_name: Robinson's Readers: Story Time and Craft

name: Knitting Foundation Class
description: Build on your knitting foundation this winter in classes led by Hilary Snell, held biweekly at Scandinavia House.

name: USPML Learn-and-Play Meetup
description: The United States Professional Mahjong League hosts an inclusive session for players to learn and practice mahjong techniques.

name: Certain Restrictions Do Apply: New Textile Works
description: A landmark textile exhibition featuring new works by Carolyn Mazloomi and Sharon Kerry-Harlan. The exhibition mines the history of Black American pioneers and expands the narrative of quiltmaking and textile art.

name: Central Harlem Walking Tour
description: The definitive tour of the heart of Harlem, exploring landmarks and the vibrant cultural legacy of Central Harlem.

name: Muddy Paws Dog Adoption Event
description: Meet wonderful dogs available for adoption from Muddy Paws Rescue. Volunteer handlers will be on-site to answer questions about the animals and the adoption process.

name: Gallery Hours
description: BioBAT Art Space is open to the public every Saturday, offering visitors a chance to explore exhibitions at the intersection of biology, technology, ecology, and design. The gallery is located within the historic Brooklyn Army Terminal and features works from artists and design innovators.

name: Penn Station & Moynihan Train Hall Tour
description: Discover the architectural history of the original Penn Station and its modern counterpart, the Moynihan Train Hall. This tour traces remnants of the 1910 masterpiece and explores the rise of the landmarks preservation movement.
short_name: Penn Station & Moynihan Train Hall Tour

name: The Saturday Jam
description: The PIT's signature Saturday evening improv jam. It's an open-to-all session where improvisers can work on their skills and play in front of a live audience in a welcoming environment.

name: Ladies Stand-Up! (Men Sit Down) Open Mic
description: A weekly stand-up open mic dedicated specifically to female and non-binary comedians to hone their craft in a supportive, focused space.

name: Adult Cooking Class — Fresh Handmade Pasta
description: Learn the art of making fresh pasta from scratch, including fettuccine and spinach ricotta ravioli paired with a classic tomato sauce. Enjoy this social, vegetarian-friendly cooking experience with your favorite wine or beer.
short_name: Adult Cooking Class — Fresh Handmade Pasta

name: Coffee Bark
description: Coffee Bark is an opportunity for Brooklyn dog owners to gather in the park for treats and coffee. It provides a community space for networking and socializing among local pet owners.

name: Audubon Nature Exploration
description: Join the Alliance for nature education programs held on weekends for all ages. These discovery sessions help visitors learn about local wildlife and the park's ecosystem.

name: Artist Studios: Jayoung Yoon
description: Artist Jayoung Yoon opens her studio doors to the public every Saturday. Experience her unique approach to contemporary art and see her works in progress.

name: Saturday Night at MFAW
location: Music for a While Listening Bar & Lounge
description: Join us for a curated evening of music and connection at this underground sanctuary. Saturday nights at MFAW transform the lounge into a vibrant tapestry of sounds, culture, and memories.

name: Gilded Age Mansions of Fifth Avenue
description: Explore the design and history of the grand urban palaces along Fifth Avenue and hear stories of the robber barons who built them.
short_name: Gilded Age Mansions of Fifth Avenue

name: Maybe Happy Ending Tickets
description: Winner of 6 Tony Awards, including Best Musical, _Maybe Happy Ending_ is the story of a chance encounter that sparks connection, adventure, and maybe even love.

name: Astoria Philosophy Book Club
description: A community-driven discussion group exploring philosophical texts and big ideas in a casual, approachable setting.

name: Best of Brooklyn Stand-Up Comedy
description: Catch top-tier stand-up featuring pro and rising stars from HBO, Comedy Central, and Netflix in an intimate setting. Guests can enjoy social board games and a variety of drinks with no two-item minimum requirement.

name: New Talent/Weekly Stand Up Auditions
description: Support the future of comedy by attending a showcase featuring promising new talent and club auditions. This show features comedians newer to the club scene in front of a real audience with no bringer fee or extra strings attached.

name: Live Jazz at Nook
description: Relax and enjoy an evening of live jazz music performed by rotating artists. This weekly series offers a smooth and sophisticated atmosphere for music lovers.

name: Screenings from The Paley Archive
description: Experience curated daily screenings of iconic media from the Paley Center's extensive archive, showcasing television history and culture. These public presentations highlight rare broadcast treasures and influential programming from decades past.

name: Volunteer Landscaping and Cleanup at Ridgewood Reservoir
description: Participate in community efforts to maintain the landscape and remove debris from the historic Ridgewood Reservoir.

name: Open Sew at The Castle
description: Join fellow sewing, quilting, and knitting enthusiasts for an afternoon of creativity and conversation. Bring your current project and enjoy a sunlit workspace with views of Little Neck Bay.

name: Second Sunday Castle Tour (1:00 PM)
description: Take a guided tour of one of NYC's finest Gothic Revival architectural treasures, originally built in 1887. Staff and volunteers lead this 45-minute exploration of the historic building.

name: Slow Play
description: Veteran improvisers take their time building a patient, detailed, and realistic world on stage.

name: Dig + Discover: Drop-in Program
description: Families can explore ancient artifacts in an immersive simulated archaeological dig site. Participants use trowels and brushes to uncover replicas of items from the museum's collection.

name: January Skills Series: French Cooking & Baking
description: Learn to master the art of French cooking and baking with free lessons hosted by Williams Sonoma. Each session focuses on a specific technique, covering everything from crepes and cassoulets to the art of the tart.

name: Sunday Worship
location: St. Paul & St. Andrew United Methodist Church
description: A weekly gathering in the sanctuary or online for a joyful, inspiring, thought-provoking, and intimate celebration of life and faith.

name: Sunday School
location: St. Paul & St. Andrew United Methodist Church
description: Weekly Sunday School for preschool and elementary children that begins in the sanctuary with an interactive message before moving to classrooms.

name: Nursery and Childcare
location: St. Paul & St. Andrew United Methodist Church
description: Professional nursery care for children ages 1-3 is available during the worship service. Check in at the front desk upon arrival for directions to the childcare room.

name: Misa en Español
description: A Spanish-language celebration of the Holy Mass held every Sunday afternoon. The service includes traditional Spanish liturgy and music, welcoming all members of the community.

name: Sunday Brunch Comedy
description: Start your Sunday afternoon with world-class comedy at the iconic Comedy Cellar on MacDougal St.

name: The Sunday Jam
description: Wind down the week with a community improv jam. Open to players of all skill levels, it's a great place to practice long-form and scene work with a friendly group.

name: Flatbush DSA Cortelyou Greenmarket Tabling
description: Engage with community members at the local greenmarket to discuss socialist initiatives and local political issues. This is a recurring outreach effort to build neighborhood power.

name: Story Time
description: A thirty-minute session celebrating books by authors and illustrators of African descent. Each session connects storytelling with mindfulness activities and themes found in the museum's permanent collection.

name: Sunday Musicians Open Mic hosted by Braden Palmer
description: Join local musicians and performers for a weekly open mic night in the intimate atmosphere of the tasting room. It is a fantastic opportunity to showcase your talent or simply enjoy the creative energy of the Industry City community.
short_name: Sunday Musicians Open Mic hosted by Braden Palmer

name: Sunday Night Karaoke with Emma
description: Join host Emma for a weekly Sunday night of karaoke at this beloved Sunset Park dive bar. Sing your favorite hits and enjoy the neighborhood's welcoming atmosphere.

name: SUNDAY Family Movie Matinee
description: Enjoy a free family-friendly movie screening every Sunday afternoon on the second floor of the Harlem Library. This weekly series is open to all ages and provides a fun community cinematic experience.

name: Community Boatbuilding
description: Participate in the construction and restoration of traditional wooden boats at the Village Community Boathouse. This hands-on workshop is open to all skill levels and focuses on teaching maritime craftsmanship, woodworking, and teamwork. Volunteers work together in a collaborative environment to maintain a fleet of heritage rowing vessels.

name: English Country Dance
description: Experience the elegant and social atmosphere of English Country Dancing, a traditional form of dance featuring live music and graceful patterns. All dances are taught, and no partner or previous experience is necessary.

name: Pink Pier
description: Experience a breathtaking all-pink waterfront takeover featuring semi-private glasshouses, cocktail chalets, and iconic Brooklyn Bridge views. This family-friendly attraction offers photo-worthy decor and specialty pink cocktails for a perfect date night or celebration.

name: Eurotea & NightWerk Sundays
description: Sundays at Playhouse feature a full day of fun starting with Eurotea and classic Showtunes, ending with the NightWerk late-night party. Perfect for tea dance lovers and drag fans.

name: Queer BIPOC Comedy Open Mic
description: A dedicated comedy stage featuring queer BIPOC performers testing new material and sharing laughs in a safe, community-focused space.

name: Stand-Up Comedy Night
description: Experience a night of world-class stand-up comedy at the legendary Comic Strip Live. Featuring a rotating lineup of professional comedians seen on major television networks.

name: Tween Primes
description: An interactive discussion series for middle schoolers featuring math-themed books and puzzles. Hosted by Dr. Arthur Benjamin, it highlights the intersection of literature and mathematical logic.

name: Open Mic (Free w/ $5 Bar Purchase!)
description: A low-stakes environment for comedians of all levels to practice new material. Free entry with a modest bar purchase.

name: Comic Book Art Class for Kids
description: A hands-on workshop for young aspiring artists to learn the basics of character design and comic book storytelling.
short_name: Comic Book Art Class for Kids

name: Committee 1: Land Use, Housing, & Economic Development
description: Meeting focused on land use, housing, economic development, business services, and transportation improvements.

name: Budget & Governmental Affairs Committee Meeting
description: Addresses rising energy costs, clean energy tax credits, and the New York State Open Meetings Law.

name: Housing Committee Meeting
description: Members discuss matters related to affordable housing, residential development, and tenant protections in the district.

name: Ladies Night
description: A supportive monthly meetup for women and nonbinary individuals of all skill levels. This event is particularly recommended for those who climb up to grade V2.

name: 50+ Climb Night
description: A monthly event for climbers aged 50 and older to connect and climb together, regardless of experience level.

name: Summer Program Information Session
description: A brief information session for prospective students interested in the YIVO-Bard Summer Program in Yiddish Studies.

name: Advanced Improv Level 2 Class Show
description: Graduates of the advanced improv level 2 class showcase their high-level improv skills.

name: Q&T Community Writing Group: Winter Schedule
description: A community writing group welcoming LGBTQ+ neighbors for hybrid and virtual writing sessions. These meetings provide a space to connect with other writers through structured writing periods and group check-ins.

name: Health, Human Services & Education Committee Meeting
description: A monthly committee meeting focusing on public health, social services, and educational programs within the Queens district.
short_name: Health, Human Services & Education Committee Meeting

name: Doodle Mic
description: A weekly open mic night where participants can showcase their talents. Please note that an item purchase is required to perform.

name: Migrant Resource Fair
location: St. Paul & St. Andrew United Methodist Church
description: A welcoming space offering food, immigration, shelter, and transportation support for migrants. Rotating resources include legal support and a pop-up free clothing store.

name: Multi-Faith Mondays
description: Weekly vigil where people of all faiths stand in solidarity for democracy. Each vigil is hosted by a different community to affirm spiritual resilience through prayer and song.

name: CQ Room Residency
description: A weekly residency featuring a rotating roster of comedians in the intimate atmosphere of the Fat Black Pussy Cat lounge.

name: New Joke Night
description: Watch professional comedians test out their newest and most experimental material for just $10.00.

name: Monday OPEN MIC! w/ Obi O'Brien
description: A weekly character-focused open mic hosted by Obi O'Brien. Performers are invited to workshop new characters, improv bits, or clowning routines in a supportive environment.

name: Mental Health Check-In! (in-person or virtual)
description: A regular mental health wellness check-in available in-person or virtually to support the LGBTQ+ community.

name: Ballroom Drop-In Practice
description: An open practice session for those participating in or interested in the ballroom and vogue scene.

name: Basketball Open Gym
description: Open court time for LGBTQ+ community members to play basketball and stay active in a safe environment.

name: Science Monday with Dr. Carol Portlock
location: New York Society for Ethical Culture
description: Every 2nd and 4th Monday, retired oncologist Dr. Carol Portlock reviews new and intriguing science across multiple fields. This session is free and open to the public, focusing on current scientific developments including health and medicine.

name: NYP Mobile Medical Unit
description: A collaboration with NewYork-Presbyterian to provide mobile health services and medical care within the park community. Services include comprehensive health screenings and medical advice.

name: Gallery Tour: Identity, Culture, and Community
description: A 45-minute docent-led tour of the newly reimagined galleries featuring more than 200 works from the museum collection. The tour explores thematic and chronological stories of Jewish identity across the diaspora.

name: Member Monday
description: Museum members are invited to enjoy exclusive after-hours access to the galleries on select Mondays.

name: Brooklyn Community Board 14 Regular Monthly Meeting
description: Regular Monthly Meeting of Brooklyn Community Board 14. Everyone is welcome! Click title for agenda and details.
short_name: Brooklyn Community Board 14 Regular Monthly Meeting

name: CONTEXT: Art, Books, & Freedom
description: Artist and curator Meg Hitchcock brings together 15 artists whose work incorporates books and text to explore visual language and freedom of expression. The artists dissect and transform books to question their authority within a revered liberal arts library.

name: Notes I Have Now and Then Made: Items from the Lydia Davis Papers
description: An exhibit showcasing the papers and materials of the writer Lydia Davis, including multiple versions of manuscripts and domestic artifacts. The collection highlights her inventive short stories and the everyday work of being a writer.
short_name: Items from the Lydia Davis Papers

name: Magma
description: An exhibition featuring work by Dominic Mangila that explores the cross-fertilization of abstraction in painting, installation, and video. Curated by Professor Tomas Vu-Daniel, this body of work was first shown at the Gwangju Biennale.

name: Knitting & Crochet (Magic) Circle
description: A communal crafting circle for fiber arts enthusiasts to work on projects together. Whether you are knitting or crocheting, join this social group to share tips and conversation.

name: Brooklyn Women's Writing Group
description: A dedicated writing session for women to find focus and community in their literary pursuits. Share your current projects and find inspiration through collective focus.

name: Maker's Meetup
description: A social gathering designed for creators, builders, and hobbyists of all kinds. Connect with other local makers and discuss ongoing projects or future ideas.

name: NYPL After School
description: A free drop-in program for children ages 6-12 featuring homework help, enrichment activities, and healthy snacks. Tutors and Teen Reading Ambassadors provide a safe, productive environment for after-school learning.

name: VJ Terry The Undead Gay Cowboy
description: On the second Monday of each month, VJ Terry plays a curated selection of classic disco and soul videos from his extensive library. Enjoy a nostalgic visual and musical journey in this welcoming Sunset Park neighborhood bar.
short_name: VJ Terry The Undead Gay Cowboy

name: Move into Poetry: A Somatic Writer's Workshop
description: A multidisciplinary workshop that incorporates breathwork, movement, and literary techniques to guide poets toward new creative pathways. Artist India Lena Gonzalez leads this somatic approach to the writing process.

name: Monday Karaoke
description: Kick off your week by taking the stage at Playhouse Bar's weekly karaoke night. Sing your favorite hits in a vibrant, welcoming atmosphere with great drink specials.

name: Karaoke Mondays
description: Take the stage every Monday night for a free community karaoke session featuring a wide selection of songs.

name: Queer Playwrights Circle
description: A collaborative workshop for queer playwrights to share scripts, receive peer feedback, and discuss theatrical craft.

name: Comedy Classes
description: Structured Monday evening training sessions led by industry veterans for aspiring comedians. Focuses on the essentials of joke writing, stage presence, and the business of comedy.

name: Economic Development Committee
description: Focuses on supporting local small businesses, labor issues, and neighborhood economic growth.

name: Housing Committee
description: Monthly discussion on affordable housing, tenant protections, and residential development projects.

name: Food Pantry Distribution
description: A twice-weekly food pantry service providing fresh food and groceries to the local community on a walk-in basis.

name: Meditation
description: Weekly morning and evening meditation sessions to help community members cultivate focus and tranquility.

name: On Your Radar
description: John Platt presents a monthly showcase featuring Joziah Longo, Lynn Hollyfield, and the Sharar Sisters.

name: Equity Planning Work Group
description: This meeting addresses equity in planning and resource distribution within the Brooklyn Community Board 16 area.

name: Queens Community Board 4 Monthly Meeting
description: Monthly meeting where board members address items of concern to the community. A portion of the meeting is reserved for the board to hear from members of the public.

name: JCTMBA Information Session
description: Join the admissions team for a virtual information session regarding the Johnson Cornell Tech MBA program.

name: Sirat
description: A father and son search for a missing relative at an endless rave in the Moroccan mountains. This journey forces them to confront their own limits in the desert.

name: PSP 2024 & 2025 Babies Meetup: The Park or the Movies?
description: Weekly Tuesday meetup for new parents and babies, either in the park or at baby-friendly cinema screenings depending on weather.

name: Sparrow's Nest X Riichi Nomi N-League Practice
description: Practice sessions for the N-League featuring Japanese Riichi Mahjong, hosted in partnership with Riichi Nomi.
short_name: Sparrow's Nest X Riichi Nomi N-League Practice

name: Committee Meeting: Transportation Landmarks and Historic Preservation
description: This committee reviews matters regarding local transit infrastructure and the preservation of historic landmarks within the district. Attendees must present identification to enter the building.

name: Arts & Culture Committee Meeting
description: This committee meeting reviews and promotes local arts initiatives, cultural events, and creative projects in the community.

name: F-Word Trivia
description: A competitive trivia night where the winning team gets $50 off their bar tab. Hosted by Sam, this monthly event usually takes place on the 4th Tuesday.

name: Pajama Prayers
location: St. Paul & St. Andrew United Methodist Church
description: Start your morning with a 30-minute virtual session of prayer and connection.

name: Weekly Happy Hour at Peculier Pub
description: A long-standing community tradition, this weekly happy hour at a historic dive bar is the perfect place to expand your social circle. Enjoy pub food and friendly conversation in a relaxed environment that welcomes both old and new members.

name: Tuesdays with Bobby Kelly
description: Bobby Kelly hosts a weekly residency at the lounge, bringing his veteran comedy insights and hilarious storytelling to the stage.

name: Hot Soup Comedy
description: One of New York City's hottest and most popular weekly stand-up showcases featuring surprise guests and top comics.

name: Transpire
description: A social and support space specifically for transgender and gender non-conforming individuals.

name: Karaoke Funhouse
description: Sing your heart out at Karaoke Funhouse hosted by Anna Copa Cabanna for a night of free vocal entertainment.

name: Bronx Community Board 6 Meeting
description: Public meetings held by Bronx Community Board 6 to discuss district concerns, land use, and municipal services. These sessions provide an opportunity for residents to engage with local governance and participate in community planning.

name: ULURP/Land Use Committee Meeting
description: Review session for land use applications, zoning changes, and urban renewal projects through the Uniform Land Use Review Procedure.

name: Bushwick Chess Club Night
description: A social chess club for players of all levels to gather and compete. Bring your own board or use those provided for a night of strategic gaming.

name: Spirited Away Psychic & Paranormal Night Winter Edition!
description: Gather with NYC’s finest readers and psychics for a glorious evening of craft cocktails and supernatural fun, hosted by The Oracle!
short_name: Spirited Away Psychic & Paranormal Night Winter Edition!

name: The Big Quiz Thing Trivia
description: Challenge your brain at Brooklyn's premier multimedia trivia night featuring professional quizmasters and competitive rounds. Gather a team and compete for bar tabs and prizes every Tuesday.

name: Trivia Night
location: Brewer's Row Beer Bar & Bottle Shop
description: Join Brewers Row for a fresh take on trivia every Tuesday. Enjoy a pint and delicious snacks while you compete.

name: The International Open Mic
description: The International Open Mic is a long-standing weekly comedy event featuring a mix of local and global talent. It takes place every Tuesday night in the venue's intimate downstairs space, the Huron Club.

name: Trivia Night with Nikki June
description: Test your knowledge every Tuesday at this competitive trivia night hosted by Nikki June. Grab a team to answer questions across multiple categories for a chance to win bar prizes and enjoy drink specials.

name: Ruby Tuesdays
description: Ruby Tuesdays brings a mix of drag performances and nightlife energy to the West Village. Join the resident queens for a night of sparkle and entertainment.

name: Wild Card Comedy
description: A weekly comedy showcase bringing a rotating lineup of talented stand-up comedians to the Alphaville stage every Tuesday.

name: Queer Writing Night
description: A supportive space for LGBTQ+ writers to work on their projects and connect with fellow creatives in a quiet, productive environment.

name: DF Open Mic/Workshop
description: A weekly Tuesday workshop and open mic session focused on the development of new comedy sets and performance techniques. Comedians of all levels come to refine their craft and timing.

name: Magic The Gathering: Commander Night
description: Join other Magic players for a casual or competitive night of Commander format gameplay. Bring your favorite 100-card deck and enjoy the social atmosphere of tabletop gaming.

name: Riftbound Nexus Night
description: Participate in Riftbound Nexus gameplay with fellow enthusiasts. A dedicated time for fans of this card game to meet and compete in a welcoming environment.

name: Senior Sessions
description: A series of engaging math workshops designed specifically for older adults. Topics range from hailstone numbers and Pythagorean puzzles to the mathematics of game theory and fractal patterns.

name: Loving Math
description: A hilariously fun online series for children in grades K-3 featuring stories, games, and laughter. Hosted by Steve Sherman, the program introduces young learners to the joy of mathematical patterns.

name: Meet a Mathematician
description: An interview series highlighting the personal stories and professional journeys of world-class mathematicians. Hosted by Dr. Arthur Benjamin, it aims to humanize STEM fields and inspire the next generation.

name: Circuit Hacking Tuesday
description: A weekly Tuesday night workshop where makers and tinkerers work together on electronics, soldering, and hardware projects.

name: Food Pantry Delivery Sorting
description: Essential volunteer sessions where community members help organize and sort food deliveries for upcoming distributions.

name: Tournament Practice Sessions
description: Weekly unranked tournament practice sessions focused on competitive Riichi Mahjong play. These sessions offer a focused environment for experienced players to hone their skills and practice tournament rulesets.

name: NYC PIT Pop Up
description: An immersive pop-up where technology, art, and community converge to showcase how design can serve justice and equity. Visitors can explore daily demos, visioning games, and multimedia installations in a shared space for co-creation.

name: Sleep Late At Night w/ Zalika!
description: A late-night DJ residency or performance series hosted by Zalika, keeping the energy high into the early hours. These Tuesday night sessions offer a curated mix of sounds for night owls.

name: SaRon Crenshaw Acoustic
description: Inspired by greats like B.B. King and Buddy Guy, SaRon is a powerful entertainer who brings his high-energy performance directly to the audience.

name: Doc / Bryan Band
description: Featuring the combined talents of Doc French and Bobby Bryan, this band delivers high-energy electric blues that keeps the venue rocking until late.

name: Blue Moon
description: A cinematic screening of the film Blue Moon as part of the Quad Cinema's regular daily schedule.

name: It Was Just An Accident
description: A screening of the cult classic film showcasing dark comedy and criminal misadventures.

name: Marty Supreme
description: Catch Marty Supreme, a new release exploring the world of competitive ping pong and mid-century Americana.

name: My Neighbor Adolf
description: A Holocaust survivor living in South America becomes convinced his neighbor is Adolf Hitler in this dramatic comedy.

name: Tuesday Night Jam Session
description: A weekly jazz jam session featuring a house band set followed by an open session for singers and instrumentalists. Musicians and patrons can enjoy complimentary vegan food and brownies.

name: Manuel Aja Espil: Ozymandias
description: An exhibition of paintings by Argentine-born Madrid-based artist Manuel Aja Espil. His introspective practice explores imaginary universes while researching the history and materiality of painting.

name: Dungeons & Dragons!
description: Gather your party for an adventurous evening of tabletop roleplaying games at the brewery. Whether you are a seasoned player or a newcomer, this weekly event brings gamers together over craft brews.

name: Six Basic Cooking Techniques
description: This foundational class teaches the six essential methods that every home cook needs to know.

name: Sheet Metal 101 : BUS (Basic Use and Safety)
description: Safety workshop covering horizontal and vertical bandsaws, drill presses, ironworkers, and grinders. Completion certifies students for independent use of metal shop equipment.

name: Watch Me Walk
description: A hilarious, biting, and compassionate new play about disability, pity, injustice, and family mythologies written and performed by Anne Gridley.

name: Senior Citizen Affairs Committee
description: An in-person meeting focused on programs, services, and advocacy for senior citizens in the community.

name: Community Resident Planning Work Group
description: Residents meet to discuss neighborhood development and planning issues directly affecting their community.

name: Transportation Committee Meeting
description: A committee meeting focused on local transit infrastructure, traffic concerns, and street safety improvements.

name: Sons of Echo
description: Curated by Daniil Simkin, this show is a bold fusion of classical and contemporary dance showcasing principal stars and groundbreaking female choreographers.

name: Hip Hop Showcase
description: A monthly showcase event highlighting rising hip hop talent in the heart of NYC. This 21+ event offers a platform for new artists to perform in a historic venue.

name: Hush Hour: A Silent Book Club at Lofty Pigeon Books
description: Enjoy a dedicated hour of quiet reading in the company of fellow book lovers. This silent book club provides a communal space for literature enthusiasts to unwind and focus on their current reads.

name: Parks & Recreation Committee
description: This committee advocates for the maintenance, accessibility, and development of public parks and green infrastructure throughout Central Harlem. It addresses concerns related to open space equity and environmental sustainability.

name: City Services & Public Safety Committee Meeting
description: Committee members discuss matters regarding sanitation, public safety, and the delivery of essential city services to local residents.
short_name: City Services & Public Safety Committee Meeting

name: We Have Fun Standup
description: A weekly standup comedy show brought to you by Dan and Zach.

name: Homework Help Tutoring
location: St. Paul & St. Andrew United Methodist Church
description: One-on-one tutoring for students in elementary school through high school provided by church volunteers.

name: Gotham Jazz with The Hot Toddies
description: The Hot Toddies Jazz Band perform an eclectic mix of hot jazz and swing perfect for dancing and enjoying the midtown skyline views.

name: Wednesdays with Jim Norton
description: Legendary comedian Jim Norton performs his residency at the lounge, offering raw and hilarious perspectives every Wednesday.

name: Wednesday Night Scene Jam!
description: A late-night improv jam focused on scene work and collaborative storytelling. Players of all experience levels are welcome to sign up and hit the stage together.

name: Foundations Of The Vajra Yoga Path
description: Learn breathing and movement practices rooted in Indo-Tibetan traditions to strengthen the body and enliven the nervous system. The class combines yoga and meditation to cultivate concentration and mindful awareness.
short_name: Foundations Of The Vajra Yoga Path

name: Jam Session Hosted By Carlos Abadie
description: A gritty, late-night Greenwich Village jam session hosted by trumpeter Carlos Abadie. Musicians from around the world gather to test their skills until the early hours of the morning.
short_name: Jam Session Hosted By Carlos Abadie

name: Trans and Gender Non-Conforming Adult Support Group
description: A weekly virtual peer support group for TGNC adults to connect and share experiences.
short_name: Trans and Gender Non-Conforming Adult Support Group

name: Community Board 6 Public Meeting
description: Public meeting sessions for Brooklyn Community Board 6 to discuss neighborhood concerns, infrastructure, and local government policy. Agendas are finalized prior to each meeting and may include land use and transportation votes.

name: Trivia!
description: Join Nook for a weekly evening of brain-teasing questions and competitive fun with friends. Test your knowledge across multiple categories to win prizes in a social atmosphere.

name: Karaoke Cocktail Club
description: Experience an evening of musical fun with weekly karaoke sessions. Grab a ginger-infused cocktail and sing your favorite hits in a supportive and high-energy setting.

name: BFF Wednesdays
description: Midweek is for best friends at Playhouse Bar. Enjoy a social atmosphere with music and cocktails during this popular weekly hump-day party.

name: Gladys Open Mic
description: A popular open mic event held on Wednesday and Friday evenings, providing a platform for comedians to test new material. It is a staple of the NYC comedy scene for discovering fresh voices.

name: RPG Encounters Public Play
description: Dive into role-playing adventures with a community of gamers. Perfect for both new players and veterans looking for a one-shot or ongoing campaign session.

name: Math Encounters
description: A public presentation series featuring world-renowned mathematicians exploring the beauty and utility of math. Topics include biological beats, randomness in physics, and the prevalence of chance in daily life.

name: Transportation Committee
description: Addressing local transit concerns, street safety, and traffic management in the community.

name: QCB5 Board Meeting
description: The Queens Community Board 5 Board Meeting is scheduled to take place. Specific details regarding the agenda will be available via the provided link.

name: Date Night: vinyl happy hour
description: A recurring midweek happy hour dedicated to the warm sounds of vinyl records. Perfect for dates or casual drinks, this series offers a relaxed analog soundtrack to your evening.

name: The Cafe Wha? House Band | Wednesday Set 1 | 9:00 pm
description: Experience the legendary Cafe Wha? House Band performing an electric mix of rock, soul, and R&B hits. This high-energy ensemble delivers an unforgettable live music experience in the heart of Greenwich Village.
short_name: The Cafe Wha? House Band | Wednesday Set 1 |

name: The Cafe Wha? House Band | Wednesday Set 2 | 10:30 pm
description: Catch the second set of the legendary house band for a late-night performance of rock and soul hits. The venue's premier ensemble keeps the energy high in one of NYC's most historic basements.
short_name: The Cafe Wha? House Band | Wednesday Set 2 |

name: Jr Mack Band
description: Junior Mack, a regular with The Allman Brothers Band and Robert Randolph, provides a soulful and captivating interpretation of electric blues.

name: Karaoke w/ Amethyst Valentino
description: A high-energy weekly karaoke night hosted by Amethyst Valentino featuring a vast song selection and neighborhood vibes.

name: Trivia with NYC Trivia League
description: Challenge your brain every Wednesday with competitive trivia hosted by the NYC Trivia League. Bring a team, enjoy some local beer, and compete for a variety of prizes.

name: Up To No God: A monthly Improv Residency
description: A monthly improv performance residency featuring creative spontaneous scenes and comedic scenarios.
short_name: Up To No God: A monthly Improv Residency

name: Hope Gardens Tenant Association Meeting
description: A recurring monthly meeting for the residents of Hope Gardens to discuss community issues and housing updates.

name: LLM in Law, Technology, and Entrepreneurship Information Session
description: Learn about the LLM program designed for lawyers looking to lead in the tech and entrepreneurship sectors.
short_name: LLM in Law, Technology, and Entrepreneurship Information Session

name: Songwriters Circle I
description: Acclaimed songwriters gather to perform their original works and share the stories behind their creative process.

name: Any Minute Now
description: A mind-bending dark comedy about an American family facing past mistakes and a doomed future as the laws of physics unravel.

name: Bipoc Meetup
description: Join the BIPOC Climbing Collective for a night focused on building community among Black, Indigenous, and People of Color. These evenings provide a dedicated space for BIPOC and queer climbers.

name: "Third Thursday" Museum Tours
description: Plan your next visit around our guided museum tours, offered on the third Thursday of the month and included with museum admission.

name: I Love You... But
description: A theatrical comedy show exploring the humorous side of love and relationships. This monthly performance blends comedy with theatrical storytelling elements.

name: Pilates
location: St. Paul & St. Andrew United Methodist Church
description: An hour-long virtual strengthening and stretching Pilates class led by Nancy M. Beginners are welcome to join the session via Zoom.

name: Storytelling Campfire
location: New York Society for Ethical Culture
description: A recurring online storytelling gathering where participants bring folk tales, family stories, and historic myths to share. This free public event celebrates the ancient tradition of oral storytelling in a virtual space.

name: Adult Cooking Class — Authentic Handmade Pasta
description: Create two unique pasta shapes—spaghetti and farfalle—from scratch while preparing roasted red pepper alfredo and nut-free pesto. This vegetarian-friendly class focuses on authentic Italian textures and sauces.
short_name: Adult Cooking Class — Authentic Handmade Pasta

name: Winter Snow in the Woodlands
description: Join a guided winter walk through the woodlands to experience the serene beauty of the park in the snow. These weekly treks focus on winter ecology and birding.

name: Karaoke Night
description: Step up to the mic for a supportive and high-energy karaoke session every Thursday. Sing your favorite pop anthems and deep cuts in a queer-friendly space that celebrates performers of all levels.

name: The Traitors Viewing & Thursday Drag
description: Join GLAM award winners Essa Noche and Mo'Riah for a viewing party of The Traitors Season 4, followed by a high-energy show with beats by DJ Jon Ali.

name: Finding Dorothy Parker
description: A theatrical production exploring the life and wit of legendary writer and poet Dorothy Parker in a series of intimate performances.

name: Commander Night
description: Join other players for a casual night of Commander, a popular multiplayer Magic: The Gathering format where players build 100-card decks around a legendary creature.

name: Lorcana Organized Play
description: Disney Lorcana fans can gather for organized matches and casual play. Build your deck and explore the magical world of Lorcana with other Illumineers.

name: Draft League - 20 Seats
description: Compete in a Magic the Gathering draft league where seats are limited to twenty players. Test your deck-building skills on the fly in this structured tournament.

name: Thursday Night Pioneer
description: A dedicated night for the Pioneer format of Magic the Gathering. Compete with your favorite decks from recent expansions in this popular constructed format.

name: Axel Tosca’s Cuban Jam
description: The Axel Tosca Trio and vocalist Xiomara Laugart host a high-energy Cuban Jam every Thursday. It is widely considered a must-see rhythmic event in the New York jazz scene.

name: The Cafe Wha? House Band | Thursday Set 1 | 9:00 pm
description: Join the Cafe Wha? House Band for their weekly Thursday residency. Known for high-energy covers and world-class musicianship, the band covers everything from classic rock to contemporary soul.
short_name: The Cafe Wha? House Band | Thursday Set 1 |

name: The Cafe Wha? House Band | Thursday Set 2 | 10:30 pm
description: Keep the night going with the second set of the Cafe Wha? House Band. This late Thursday slot features the same high-octane performance in an intimate, historic atmosphere.
short_name: The Cafe Wha? House Band | Thursday Set 2 |

name: Open Decks w/ DJ TK
description: Aspiring DJs and music lovers are invited to bring their own music and showcase their skills during this open deck night.

name: Hubby Jenkins
description: Talented multi-instrumentalist Hubby Jenkins explores traditional blues and roots music, drawing on his experience as a busker and former member of the Carolina Chocolate Drops.

name: T Blues Band
description: The world-class house band of Terra Blues features rotating lineups of masterful guitarists and vocalists performing top-tier electric blues.

name: Matt and Ben
description: A comedic play examining the friendship and creative journey of Matt Damon and Ben Affleck.

name: Thursday Happy Hour w/ Steven Crammer
description: Early evening happy hour jazz sessions led by drummer Steven Crammer and resident guests. Tips for the musicians are encouraged during these intimate sets.

name: The Gotham All-Stars
description: A professional Showcase featuring comics from Netflix, Showtime, HBO, The Daily Show, and other major networks and films.

name: Thesaurus Rex
description: High-energy late-night performance by the versatile band Thesaurus Rex.

name: Los Cumpleaños
description: A psychedelic take on classic Colombian Cumbia, Porro, and Salsa Criolla with new wave synths and retro organ sounds.

name: Shuffle
description: A new release documentary feature directed by Benjamin Flaherty with a runtime of 82 minutes.

name: CRUX Newbie Night
description: Designed for new LGBTQ+ climbers, CRUX hosts teach participants about equipment, safety, and climbing techniques.

name: Liquid Crystal Respawn
description: An exhibition investigating the posthuman as sensorial and affective, defending emotional life within the liquid crystal display.

name: Autumn Knight: NOTHING: more
description: An anti-still life performance and moving composition of objects and bodies exploring the process of making and remaking the self.

name: Brooklyn Contra Dance
description: A high-energy community dance featuring live traditional music and a caller who teaches all the moves. No partner or prior experience is required, and each event begins with an introductory lesson.

name: Drag Race Viewing Party
description: Watch the latest episode of RuPaul's Drag Race with a lively local crowd followed by late-night DJ sets and dancing. This weekly event is a staple for the queer community looking to enjoy the show in an inclusive bar setting.

name: Drag Race Playhouse & Play Fridays
description: Friday nights start with RuPaul's Drag Race viewing followed by the explosive 'Assassins' drag show. The night continues with Play Fridays, a late-night dance party.

name: Friday Night Drafts
description: Weekly Friday Night Magic drafts where players can build decks from booster packs on the fly. Pods of 8 are formed and start playing as soon as they are full.

name: Magic The Gathering: Friday Night Magic
description: Celebrate the weekend with Friday Night Magic, the staple event for the MTG community. Join for matches, camaraderie, and casual gaming fun spanning various formats.
short_name: Magic The Gathering: Friday Night Magic

name: Flesh & Blood Armory
description: Join the Flesh and Blood community for weekly Armory events. Battle it out in this popular trading card game to win exclusive prizes and climb the local rankings.

name: Folding Fridays
description: A weekly origami series that explores the mathematical properties of paper folding. Participants of all ages can join this online session to create complex geometric structures from simple paper squares.

name: End of the Line Comedy Show
description: A late-night comedy showcase featuring high-energy sets to wrap up the work week.
short_name: End of the Line Comedy Show

name: The Cafe Wha? House Band | Friday Set 1 | 9:00 pm
description: Kick off your weekend with the premier house band in Greenwich Village. This Friday night set features the full ensemble playing rock, R&B, and soul favorites that have made this venue a legend.
short_name: The Cafe Wha? House Band | Friday Set 1 |

name: The Cafe Wha? House Band | Friday Set 2 | 11:45 pm
description: Experience the ultimate late-night Friday performance at Cafe Wha?. The house band takes the stage for a midnight-adjacent set of high-energy rock and soul to fuel your NYC night.
short_name: The Cafe Wha? House Band | Friday Set 2 |

name: RuPaul's Drag Race Season 18 Viewing Party
description: A free weekly viewing party in the front room to catch all the Season 18 drama and runway looks.
short_name: RuPaul's Drag Race Season 18 Viewing Party

name: The Kings of Karaoke
description: Late-night karaoke party every weekend where you can channel your inner rock star with a massive library of songs.

name: Friday Happy Hour w/ Peter Watrous
description: Start the weekend with a happy hour jazz session led by guitarist and writer Peter Watrous and various musical partners. Enjoy a one-drink minimum entry for world-class jazz.

name: Queens Trivia Night
description: Put your knowledge to the test every Friday night at the Queens taproom with competitive trivia. Enjoy fresh craft beer while competing for prizes in a fun and social atmosphere.

name: An Ark
description: This world-premiere theatrical encounter is the first play created for mixed reality, featuring an ensemble cast including Ian McKellen and Golda Rosheuvel. Four guides shepherd the audience through a deeply personal exploration of the human condition, using mixed reality glasses to speak directly to you.

name: Iconic Views of Central Park Tour
description: Visit the iconic landmarks and landscapes that make Central Park the world’s most famous park. Explore the architecture and scenery that define the heart of Manhattan's green space.
short_name: Iconic Views of Central Park Tour

name: Ascent
description: Reach new heights with Ascent, the world's largest external all-glass elevator, offering incredible views of the NYC skyline from outside the building.

name: SUMMIT Premium Experience
description: Elevate your visit with exclusive priority access, an unforgettable 90-minute guided tour, and a digital photo package to document your trip to the top.

name: Video of “Stories Around the Table” Filmed Oct 28, 2025
description: A video recording of the "Stories Around the Table" event, capturing personal narratives and community storytelling originally filmed in October 2025.
short_name: Video of “Stories Around the Table” Filmed Oct 28, 2025

name: Victoria Horne: Mood Indigo
description: A video installation featuring the work of Victoria Horne, captured by Mourrice Papi, exploring the theme of "Mood Indigo."

name: Sinatra Under The Stars
description: Join Jesse Posa every Friday for a tribute to Sinatra under the stars from 11 PM to close, offering a mix of live music and classic crooner vibes.

name: Wunderkammer III
description: This exhibition in the Lobby Gallery features a 'cabinet of curiosities' showcasing diverse objects from the museum's permanent collection. It remains accessible to the public while the main museum prepares for its next installation.

name: Speak Out! Student Poster Exhibit
description: Learn from the inspiring leadership of the fifth graders who participated in the inaugural Speak Out! Poster Contest.

name: Clearly Indigenous: Native Visions Reimagined in Glass
location: National Museum of the American Indian
description: This exhibition showcases how Native artists have pushed the boundaries of glass art to tell stories and interpret traditional designs in new, vibrant ways.

name: Litany of the Sacred Heart
description: Participate in this solemn Friday evening prayer dedicated to the Sacred Heart of Jesus. The litany expresses devotion to the compassionate love and mercy of Christ.

name: Vaginal Davis Magnificent Product
description: A major exhibition of Vaginal Davis, spanning five decades of her practice as a performer, visual artist, author, filmmaker, musician, and educator. The presentation spotlights Ms. Davis’s role as an underground trailblazer in culture and queer politics.

name: Inuuteq Storch Soon Will Summer Be Over
description: This exhibition features photography by Inuuteq Storch, exploring Greenlandic identity and landscapes. The work captures the relationship between personal memory and the environment through evocative imagery.
short_name: Inuuteq Storch Soon Will Summer Be Over

name: Four Dilations
description: Four artistic positions that counter the dominant ways in which time is kept today. The exhibition's title references temporal dilation, exploring how time passes at different rates based on distinct frames of reference.

name: Ayoung Kim Delivery Dancer Codex
description: The US debut of Ayoung Kim's Delivery Dancer trilogy, featuring video installations that use generative AI and videogame engines. The narratives collide geopolitics, synthesize mythologies, and interrogate modern technologies.

name: Gabrielle Goliath Personal Accounts
description: The US premiere of Personal Accounts, a video series responding to the impacts of patriarchal violence. The work documents survivor testimonials by portraying breaths, sighs, and moments of silence between spoken words.

name: Homeroom: LA ESCUELA___ Education as Resistance
description: Artist-run platform LA ESCUELA___ initiates a collective learning project charting pedagogies from across Latin America. The project explores education as a form of social and political resistance.

name: Rewind by Whisbe
description: A contemporary installation by WhIsBe featuring oversized childhood toys displayed at the South Oculus Plaza.

name: Sushi Making 101
description: Master the basics of sushi making, including rice preparation and rolling techniques with Chef Shota. This hands-on class is perfect for beginners looking to create their own rolls at home.

name: Classic and Creative Cocktails
description: Shake things up with Chef Carolyn as you learn to create both timeless classics and innovative new cocktails. Master the balance of flavors and professional garnishing techniques.

name: Recycle Your Tree
description: Help Battery Park City stay green by depositing your undecorated tree at the curb for collection. Parks staff will pick up trees to be chipped and used for mulch throughout the local parks.

name: Highlights Tour of the Collection
description: Daily guided tours introducing the campus, J. Pierpont Morgan’s library, and selections from the permanent collection.

name: Exhibition | Giovanni Bellini's "Pietà" Restored
description: An exhibition showcasing Bellini’s early Renaissance masterpiece, on view in the U.S. for the first time following restoration.

name: Exhibition | Caravaggio's "Boy with a Basket of Fruit" in Focus
description: A special presentation of Caravaggio's early masterpiece, on loan from the Galleria Borghese in Rome.

name: Exhibition | Renoir Drawings
description: An exploration of Renoir's drawing practice, showing how he used paper to test ideas and interpret human and landscape forms.

name: Got To Be There: The Apollo, Its People, and Its Stories
description: Step behind the curtain of the world-famous Apollo Theater in this exhibition located at The Victoria’s Laura & Frank Baker Gallery showcasing its people and stories.
short_name: The Apollo, Its People, and Its Stories

name: Dry January
description: Discover zero-proof cocktails and mocktails across various Seaport venues throughout the month of January. Reset your system and quench your thirst at local bars and world-class restaurants.

name: Alpine Luxe: The Ski Chalet Experience at Industry Kitchen
description: Industry Kitchen transforms into a winter ski chalet with seasonal décor and a specialized menu. This experience provides a cozy escape with sweeping waterfront views.

name: Bjorn Amelan: Recent Works
description: An exhibition featuring recent visual works by Bjorn Amelan, showcasing the artist's creative evolution and new artistic expressions.

name: MAD for a Cause: Kid Zone Coat Drive
description: A community support initiative collecting coats for those in need through the Kid Zone program. Visitors are encouraged to donate new or gently used coats at the museum.
short_name: MAD for a Cause: Kid Zone Coat Drive

name: Ragtime
description: RAGTIME is a sweeping musical adaptation of E.L. Doctorow’s novel that follows three fictional families in pursuit of the American Dream at the dawn of the 20th Century.

name: Glorious Grand Central Terminal
location: Vanderbilt Hall at Grand Central Terminal
description: Discover the secrets of this Beaux-Arts masterpiece, from its hidden whispering gallery to the site where TV news was born. Learn about the building's design, its innovative engineering, and the fight to save it from destruction.

name: Boat Banter on the Barge
description: Join professional sailor and educator Margaret Flanagan for a casual conversation about boats and waterfront management. This low-key session invites visitors to share maritime stories and discuss harbor navigation.

name: Vermicomposting: Worm Bins 101
description: Build and care for your own worm bin to turn kitchen scraps into nutrient-dense soil.

name: Pay-What-You-Can Creative Writing Winter Workshops
description: Creative writing and art workshops for kids ages 8-18 to experience safe and creative spaces for their stories. All workshops are accessible through a pay-what-you-can model for all families.

name: New York Safe Boating Course
description: A free course designed to teach essential skills for navigating safely and responsibly on the water. Participants can earn their New York State Boating Safety Certificate through this training partnership with The Nautical School.

name: Art Cart: Münter
description: Weekly communal art making in the galleries for visitors of all ages to explore works on view.

name: Laser Cutter Orientation
description: An essential orientation for members to learn the safe operation and best practices for the space's laser cutting equipment.

name: Group Exhibition: Dimensions of Art & Yuriy Dikov: Presence with Future & Past
description: This collaborative group exhibition features Dimensions of Art alongside Yuriy Dikov's Presence with Future & Past, exploring themes of time and form. The collection includes paintings, sculptures, installations, and digital art by artists such as Alex Katsenelinboigen, Grigory Gurevich, Sasha Dikov, and Zura.
short_name: Dimensions of Art & Yuriy Dikov: Presence

name: Improv Comedy. Eight Is Never Enough
description: High-energy musical improv comedy show where every scene is made up on the spot. Great for families and fans of interactive theater.
short_name: Improv Comedy. Eight Is Never Enough

name: Gorg Saturdays & Shenanigans
description: Experience the ultimate Saturday night with a lineup of 'Gorg' drag shows and high-energy 'Shenanigans'. Dance to pop hits and house music all night long.

name: New Talent Show
description: A high-energy Saturday showcase featuring the most promising rising stars on the comedy circuit. Discover tomorrow's headliners as they perform their best material in a historic setting.

name: MSM Precollege Performances
description: Weekly performances highlighting the exceptional talent of students in the Manhattan School of Music Precollege program.

name: Refuge Saturdays
description: A weekly Saturday night dance series featuring underground electronic music in a 200-year-old industrial warehouse. The events showcase a custom 4-point sound system and local and international selectors.

name: Weekly Masc4Mask PCR Testing
description: Partnering with the mutual aid group Masc4Mask, the bookstore provides free rapid PCR testing on-site every week.

name: The Cafe Wha? House Band | Saturday Set 1 | 9:00 pm
description: Saturday nights at Cafe Wha? are legendary for a reason. Join the house band for a prime-time set of iconic covers and infectious energy in a world-famous basement club.
short_name: The Cafe Wha? House Band | Saturday Set 1 |

name: The Cafe Wha? House Band | Saturday Set 2 | 11:45 pm
description: The quintessential NYC late-night experience features the Cafe Wha? House Band until the early morning hours. Expect a high-octane mix of rock, R&B, and classic soul hits.
short_name: The Cafe Wha? House Band | Saturday Set 2 |

name: Juilliard Student Recitals
description: A series of free solo and studio recitals featuring Juilliard's talented student instrumentalists. These performances showcase a range of classical works on piano, flute, violin, viola, and percussion as part of the students' professional training.

name: Bobby Bryan Trio
description: A regular fixture on the NYC scene, Bobby Bryan brings his latest musical incarnation to life in this dynamic trio setting.

name: SaRon Crenshaw Band
description: Experience SaRon Crenshaw's full electric band as they perform high-impact blues inspired by legends like Albert Collins and Stevie Ray Vaughan.

name: Lloyd the DJ
description: Resident DJ manager Lloyd brings the house down with a multi-genre set. Expect a mix of dancehall, reggae, soca, hip-hop, and pop hits to keep the energy high all night long.

name: Saturday Duet Sets w/ Adam Kolker
description: Intimate Saturday evening jazz duet performances featuring saxophonist Adam Kolker performing with a rotating lineup of guest pianists and instrumentalists.

name: Reading in Italian | New Session
location: Italian Cultural Institute of New York
description: Children between the ages of 6 and 12 are invited to participate in weekly Italian reading sessions featuring a curated selection of books. Each session focuses on improving language skills and fostering a connection to Italian culture.

name: Member Mornings: Monet and Venice
description: Enjoy exclusive morning access to the Monet and Venice exhibition before the museum opens to the general public. This event is a special benefit for museum members.

name: Weekly Storytime
description: Join us every Saturday morning for a fun and engaging storytime session for children. Each week features different books and activities to spark young imaginations.

name: Authentic Tagliatelle al Pesto
description: Learn the traditional methods of making fresh tagliatelle and classic pesto from scratch with Chef Lyuba. You'll master dough stretching and the art of the perfect sauce consistency.

name: Classic French Macarons
description: Master the delicate art of French macaron making, including piping and filling techniques with Chef Su. Learn how to achieve the perfect macaron foot and flavor-packed centers.

name: Saturday Figure Drawing
location: Museum of Illustration at the Society of Illustrators
description: Hosted by Johnny Dombrowski, these figure drawing sessions invite artists of all levels to sketch from live models. Guests can also enjoy a light brunch and bottomless mimosas with museum admission included.

name: Mozzarella Making
description: A hands-on, family-friendly workshop where kids learn the art of stretching and shaping fresh mozzarella.

name: Volunteer Information Session
description: Learn how to get involved in Prospect Park, whether you are a new visitor or a long-time supporter interested in community service and park maintenance.

name: QED Presents
description: QED's flagship stand-up showcase featuring a curated lineup of professional comedians from late-night TV and the national circuit.

name: Xanadu Day Disco (skate)
description: Enjoy a daytime roller skating session featuring disco beats and a vibrant atmosphere for skaters of all levels. These sessions offer a groove-filled afternoon perfect for starting your weekend on wheels.

name: Jangueando: Recent Acquisitions, 2021-2025
description: An ongoing exhibition showcasing the most recent additions to El Museo del Barrio's permanent collection. The show highlights contemporary voices and diverse perspectives within the Latinx and Latin American artistic landscape.

name: Visit The Morbid Anatomy Library and Gift Shop in Brooklyn, New York
description: Explore an extensive collection of books on death, anatomical art, and the occult, alongside artifacts and curiosities from around the world. The on-site gift shop features rare objects, obscure tarot decks, and Mexican retablos.
short_name: Visit The Morbid Anatomy Library and Gift Shop in Brooklyn, New York

name: Understanding Anger – A Trauma-Informed Approach
description: This two-part series explores different types of anger and their hidden wisdom, helping you discern which angers you can work with in meditation and which need specialized support.

name: The Anatomy of a Movie Poster: The Work of Dawn Baillie
description: A pop-up exhibition of original movie posters in partnership with Poster House, celebrating the award-winning work of artist Dawn Baillie. Visitors can explore the creative process and history behind iconic cinema imagery.

name: Greetings from Staten Island: A Postcard Tour
description: Explore Staten Island’s past through vintage postcards from the William McMillen Collection. This exhibition compares early 20th-century views to the modern day, highlighting the island's history as an industrial and vacation destination.

name: Shoes: Two Centuries of Style and Function
description: Discover 200 years of changing fashion, labor, and identity through this footwear exhibit. It features over 25 original pairs of shoes ranging from 19th-century wedding slippers to 1970s sneakers.
short_name: Two Centuries of Style and Function

name: Winter Guided Tours
description: Expert historical interpreters lead a walking tour through restored historic buildings furnished with artifacts from the 18th and 19th centuries. Tours are offered on weekends and special holidays throughout the winter season.

name: (No) Refunds
description: Theaterlab presents (No) Refunds, a theatrical production running in mid-January 2026.

name: Pole Before Dark! An Early Evening Pole Dancing Showcase
description: An early evening showcase of local pole dancing talent, held every third Saturday of the month.
short_name: Pole Before Dark! An Early Evening Pole Dancing Showcase

name: 45 Years of Pac-Man
description: Celebrate 45 years of PAC-MAN with an immersive exhibit, arcade gameplay, and specialized gaming sessions in the PaleyGX studio. This museum-wide event highlights the cultural legacy and evolution of gaming's most iconic character.

name: PaleyGX Tournament Featuring Fortnite
description: Compete in an action-packed Fortnite tournament or attend dedicated 'Tips & Tricks' workshops for both adults and children. These sessions are designed to build skills and foster community among competitive and casual gamers alike.

name: Sewing Machine : BUS (Basic Use and Safety)
description: Introduction to the safe operation and setup of professional sewing machines. Students will learn threading, tension adjustment, and machine maintenance for textile projects.

name: CNC Embroidery Basics
description: Learn to transfer digital SVG designs onto fabric using CNC embroidery technology. This workshop covers file preparation and machine manipulation to add texture to projects.

name: Sunday Mass at St. Joseph’s
description: Weekly Sunday mass open to the public and the student community in Greenwich Village.

name: A Handmade Palette: Natural and Historical Watercolors
description: Artist Natalie Stopka teaches the history and practice of paint-making in this two-session workshop. Participants learn to concoct a palette of 12 handmade watercolors using pre-industrial recipes.

name: Sunday Funday Family Zone
description: A monthly drop-in activity for museum members that combines physics and fun through themed winter challenges. This month's session focuses on building snowball catapults to explore force and motion.

name: Christianity 101
location: St. Paul & St. Andrew United Methodist Church
description: A series of after-church sessions for any level of familiarity with Christianity, covering history, holy days, theology, and scripture.

name: Birding in Peace
description: Experience the serenity of the cemetery during a peaceful morning bird-watching tour. Participants can observe the diverse avian life that calls the cemetery home in a quiet, early-morning environment.

name: Ladies' Mic

The film is followed by a conversation with the filmmaker about the creative process and the historical narratives involved.

name: Mae Martin: The Possum

name: The Dollop - The Dollop Podcast Live

name: Tennis Courts, Paper Lady, Range Life

name: Evening Lecture Series: Michelle Segre

name: Eunbi Kim: it feels like a dream

name: Skeete

name: Shimza

name: Okonski

name: Terror Terroir

name: Conference in honor of Souleymane Bachir Diagne
description: A multi-day academic conference celebrating the intellectual legacy and philosophical contributions of Souleymane Bachir Diagne.
short_name: Conference in honor of Souleymane Bachir Diagne

name: Ranger Trucco
description: One of tech house's fastest rising names, Ranger Trucco, performs a set of heavy grooves and original vocals.

name: Daniel Donato: Cosmic Country
description: Daniel Donato brings his 'Cosmic Country' sound to the Beacon Theatre, merging traditional honky-tonk with the improvisational spirit of jam bands. It's a night of virtuosic guitar work and modern Americana.

name: 49th & Main
description: Irish indie-pop duo 49th & Main performs a headline set featuring their blend of house-influenced pop music.

name: Angel Du$t
description: Hardcore supergroup Angel Du$t performs live with support from Outta Pocket, Jivebomb, and Vanity for a high-intensity evening of punk.

name: Bywater Call
description: A powerful 7-piece band blending southern soul, gospel, and blues-rock into a high-energy live experience.

name: morning dew: cyanotype & natural toners

name: City Pop Waves: Masayoshi Takanaka Super Takanaka World Live 2026

name: Son de Cuba

name: April Fools Comedy Jam Presented by Rip Micheals
