name: Mannes Sounds: Pianists at the Opera
description: A donor appreciation concert exploring 19th-century grand opera through the lens of virtuoso piano techniques. Curated by Donald Wagner, the concert highlights the piano's ability to capture operatic grandeur.

name: Tom Paine's War: Thomas Paine in the American Revolution - Liberty 250® Lecture
description: Author Jack Kelly explores the life and work of Thomas Paine, whose pamphlets Common Sense and The American Crisis rallied the nation toward independence. The lecture examines Paine's time on the front lines and his impact on Revolutionary morale.
short_name: Thomas Paine in the American Revolution - Liberty 250® Lecture

name: Est-ce que je vis encore? Two 18th-century Women and Literature
description: Kate Tunstall delivers a talk on the powers of literature to grant an afterlife to 18th-century female writers and the costs associated with it.
short_name: Est-ce que je vis encore? Two 18th-century Women and Literature

name: Masterpieces of Skin: Medieval Manuscripts on Parchment and Vellum
description: Art historian Brenda Edgar traces the history of handmade books crafted from animal skin. This session explores illuminated manuscripts as both sacred texts and tactile physical objects featuring quirks of flesh and hair.
short_name: Medieval Manuscripts on Parchment and Vellum

name: Women on the Move: Judy Batalion on The Last Woman of Warsaw
description: National Jewish Book Award winner Judy Batalion discusses her debut novel about two Jewish women in Warsaw facing the danger of war. She explores the connection between historical research on resistance fighters and the process of writing fiction.
short_name: Judy Batalion on The Last Woman of Warsaw

name: Wasted x Van Alen
description: Pratt Institute visits Van Alen for their annual public conversation exploring the status of waste in architecture and the potential for reuse with minimal energy and capital expenditure.

name: The Albertine Book Club Reads "Free Day" by Inès Cagnati
description: A book club discussion of Inès Cagnati's novel Free Day, a translated French classic published by The New York Review of Books.
short_name: The Albertine Book Club Reads "Free Day" by Inès Cagnati

name: Evening Lecture Series: Elise Ansel
description: Artist Elise Ansel speaks about her creative process and artistic work as part of the spring Evening Lecture Series.

name: El Cuarteto De Nos
description: The acclaimed Uruguayan rock band El Cuarteto De Nos brings their distinctive style and lyrical depth to Brooklyn. They are widely recognized for their satirical lyrics and genre-blending sound.

name: Power, Public Space, and Democracy
description: An exploration of the vital role public space plays in the expression of power and the health of modern democratic societies.

name: Robert Plant
location: Cathedral of St. John the Divine
description: Rock legend Robert Plant performs in the majestic setting of the Cathedral, featuring collaborations with Suzi Dian and Rosie Flores.

name: Kerry Ellis & Ben Forster In Concert
description: Two of the biggest stars of the West End and Broadway perform together in a night of musical theater favorites.
short_name: Kerry Ellis & Ben Forster In Concert

name: Golda Schultz Sings Barber & Stravinsky
location: David Geffen Hall at Lincoln Center
description: Soprano Golda Schultz joins conductor Kwamé Ryan for Samuel Barber’s Knoxville: Summer of 1915. The program also features the world premiere of a new work by George Lewis.

name: Bell X1 & Dowry Strings
description: Multi-platinum-selling indie rock trio Bell X1 performs new interpretations of their 25-year catalog. They are joined by the cross-genre ensemble Dowry Strings for this anticipated return to New York.

name: Monthly Jazz Jam - April 2026 - Honoring Billie Holiday
description: Join the Flushing Town Hall jazz community for a tribute to the legendary Billie Holiday. Musicians of all backgrounds are welcome to perform and improvise together during this monthly jam session.

name: New Directors/New Films 2026
description: Jointly presented with MoMA, this prestigious festival showcases emerging directors at the vanguard of cinema, continuing its long history of discovering major global talent.

name: kwn
description: An intimate performance showcasing the soulful R&B sounds of emerging artist kwn.

name: Bird and Nature Walk: Freshkills-North Park
description: Witness the return of spring migrants in the unique landscape of Freshkills Park. The walk focuses on identifying raptors and migrating waterfowl within this significant environmental restoration project.

name: Jarlath Regan: Gas Man
description: Acclaimed Irish comedian and podcast host Jarlath Regan performs two shows of his latest stand-up special, 'Gas Man'.

name: The Best One Yet Podcast
description: Join the hosts of The Best One Yet for a live podcast recording featuring insightful commentary and engaging stories.

name: Evening Lecture Series: Jonathan D. Katz
description: Jonathan D. Katz presents a lecture titled 'On The First Homosexuals: The Birth of a New Identity 1869-1939'.
short_name: Evening Lecture Series: Jonathan D. Katz

name: Spring Celebration & Fundraiser
description: A festive spring gathering and fundraiser dedicated to supporting the academic and artistic programming of The Latinx Project.

name: Hannah Lillith Assadi Discusses Paradiso 17
description: Novelist Hannah Lillith Assadi discusses her intimate, sweeping tale of a man’s search for home across the world.

name: Go Kurosawa
description: Multi-instrumentalist and Kikagaku Moyo co-founder Go Kurosawa performs his psychedelic and experimental solo compositions.

name: Jesse Malin: Almost Grown

name: My Excellency — With Jonathan Cohen & Gy Mirano

name: Jim Gaffigan: Everything is Wonderful! Tour
description: Seven-time Grammy-nominated comedian Jim Gaffigan stops at the Beacon Theatre with his new world tour. Known for his relatable observational humor about family and food, Gaffigan remains one of the world's top stand-up performers.

name: kwn tour 2026

name: Bluegrass Improvisation for All Instruments with Stash Wyslouch - A Workshop

name: The Belair Lip Bombs, Dust

name: Mannes Sounds: Romance is in the Air

name: Luke Waldron

name: Naïka - Eclesia Tour

name: Machine Girl - PsychoWarrior Tour

name: mclusky

name: The Wedding March

name: The Morris Brothers Book Release Party

description: A book launch event for "The Morris Brothers," the first volume in the "Bronx Civil Rights Icons" series, featuring a talk by editor Dr. Steven Payne.

name: Peyton Corinne: Unbound

name: Four Daughters (Les Filles d'Olfa)

name: Mike Doughty

name: Natalia Lafourcade

name: Steinza

name: Shadowgrass

name: WMI presents Saha Gnawa x El Khat

name: McDonalds Gospelfest

name: Earth Day How-To Festival

name: Chris Grey: Paradise Lost Tour
description: Emerging artist Chris Grey brings his "Paradise Lost Tour" to NYC for a night of emotive and powerful music.

name: Verka Serduchka & Band

name: Alice Phoebe Lou: Oblivion Tour

name: West Point Band Free Concert
location: Lehman Center for the Performing Arts
description: The West Point Band performs a special free concert featuring a diverse program of band literature, classical selections, and patriotic favorites.

name: Mali Obomsawin

name: Dvořák’s Seventh & Sibelius’s Violin Concerto

name: Cauvery Madhavan in Conversation

name: Sir Chloe

name: Candlelight: The Best of Bollywood and Tollywood on Strings
description: Discover the vibrant music of Bollywood and Tollywood reimagined through classical string performances under the glow of candlelight.

name: The Cantorial “Golden Age” in America

location: The Sheen Center for Thought & Culture

description: A musical tribute program held at the historic Schomburg Center in honor of Arturo Schomburg's legacy.

description: Korey Coleman and the Double Toasted crew bring their unique blend of movie reviews and commentary to a live audience.

name: Laci Mosley: Scam Goddess Live

name: Denim & Pearls: Birthday Comedy Extravaganza
